transcript.text: There were notes.
think Panzar got the notes, which was helpful at least, but yeah, it was disappointing for her.
All right.
I actually wanted to start the meeting off by introducing Matthew.
He's our chief content officer, but he is very much concerned with content quality for our clients and also improving the existing workflows to make them work better for you.
Okay.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Great to meet you.
Nice to meet you.
Yeah.
As Aida mentioned, like I'm all about the workflows, making sure that the pipelines are set up well.
You you folks are kind of coming out of the sprint process where we're like, hey, what do we want to do for you?
And trying to get an understanding of the company.
I have watched all those calls and read all of your documentation.
I am also familiar with the company.
I think we announced your series B on TechCrunch by 2021.
But I am Matthew Panzarino.
previously ran TechCrunch.
I was the editor-in-chief from 2013 to 2023.
Thank you.
For about 10 years, serving editorial operations there across, of course, our news gathering org, then TechCrunch Disrupt, Startup Battlefield, and dozens of other editorial products.

So I joined up at GrowthX because I got to talking with Daniel and Marcel, got connected with them, and we were thinking the same way about the animization of content and how AI can sort of enable people to produce quality content at scale, thinking about the breaking down of the components that make up quality content, and understanding and articulating how we can enable people to do that faster and better with AI.
So that's why I'm here, and why I'm here with you is to kind of make sure that this pipeline that we've created gets productized well to smoothly deliver content for you.
I know you've talked a little bit about wanting to increase cadence of delivery, which we can do, which is to make sure that the wheels are oiled and running smoothly.
The gears are interlocked properly, and that's my job, so I'm going to be generating content for you, editing it.
I'm presenting it to you over the next couple of weeks and making sure that you're really happy with it and making sure any tweaks needed that are necessary get done.
So that's why I'm here.
Pleasure meet you.
Nice.
All right.
Perfect.
So I wanted to kick things off by just giving you a quick content update.
So last week we delivered four new blogs.
You have reviewed them.
The plan is to resolve any outstanding comments, incorporate them into the artifacts, and then we're going to publish the four this week.
Yesterday I had a really nice conversation with Ashish, your CPO.
He gave me a lot of good insights on the engineering audience.
And there's significant, like, there's some overlap, but it gave us a good overview into, like, some of the differences between the audiences, notably how engineers.
you.
Thank Thank Thank
Often deal with higher volumes of work than architects, so that's one thing that was a really big takeaway.

He did mention that, you know, for more sort of granular insights, it would be helpful to speak to someone from sales, and I know that sales teams are usually really busy, but I was wondering if you're using Outreach or Gong or any type of other platform that has some recordings that you could share with us, or at least transcripts or summaries.
Yeah, we are using Gong, so there's a ton of call scripts.
What do you want?

So it would be helpful to get a couple of good calls where we just can get a better understanding of the engineers' pain points and why they come to Monograph, and like, when they're talking to your sales team, like, what are the issues that they bring up, what are the problems that they want to solve, things like that.
Yeah.
Or like, what's lacking in their currency?
Like if they're using Google Sheets, we want to know why that doesn't work specifically.
Or if they're using a different tool, like we also want to understand like why they hate it.
Yeah.
Chris, can you do me a favor and just like ping Sam, Sam R, and grab any type of Gong recordings that are engineering specific and then pass that over?
Sure thing.
So how much is useful for you?
Are you thinking like three, because you listed spreadsheet and then like a competing tool.
Can you help us?
Because we can isolate down to segment and we can also isolate down potentially to competing solution.
But give me a sense for like, if you were to spec out what's the minimum you want to know, that's going to be insightful.
How would you describe it?
Yeah.
Panzar, do you want to weigh in?
I think you have probably a good idea of like the workflow and how much context it needs.
Yeah, I think in terms of pain points here, this would inform personas and.
And also the company context for us.
Those are the two primary artifacts that we're going to.
What we want to know is just keep things simple.
What are the top three things that in your top three solutions engineers are moving away from and towards Monograph?
In other words, what are the top three things they hate and don't like?
And whether that's the form of a solution or a major pain point, those are the insights we'd like to glean.
And we need to start there.
So as one example, do they live in Sheets and they hate it?
Or do they use an additional tool, but it doesn't do, for instance, like personnel time management, but it does do payments management.
And they like one solution for both.
That sort of thing.
So one thing we could do is like I could take all of our closed one engineering customers that I could take three that were spreadsheets and three that were competing tools.
And we could give you the SDR call and the disco call.
Sounds good.
Yeah, that's perfect.
Just a quick question.
Chris, do we need to filter down?
Because I'm going to make an assumption here, Aida and Matthew, that whatever we give you, you're going to data dump it into some type of AI model to summarize those calls anyway.
So we should probably index on more versus pre-selecting out specific calls.
Yeah, I actually have API access, so we could try to hook up like a stream of filter, you know, by the segment.
That's a good question.
I haven't done this kind of thing.
Actually, you know, I have.
I have done this thing, this kind of thing at scale before and parsed about 150 or so.
So, like, I don't think there's any hard limit.
I would say out beyond a dozen, it's diminishing returns.
Like, because I think you'll find...
If a lot of pattern matching, I doubt that there's, you know, 150 different permutations of painting points.
It's most likely going to distill pretty quickly.
So I would say maybe a dozen, and you can just do 50-50.
know, 50% people are coming from manual raw labor solutions that they're doing.
You know, I built a spreadsheet in my basement.
And then the other 50% being, hey, we used a competitor's tools or combination of competing tools, and we didn't like them because of XYZ.
We do have this page.
Chris, are you aware?
Yeah.
Because all the gone recordings are all here, but it looks like they're all demo calls.
So like, they're all essentially first call touches from an AE.
They're not like closing calls or anything else.
If you're just trying to get context of what an engineer might say or might talk about, this might give you enough without filtering down.
So like, well, what, what do they need to hear at the closing?
Um,
Typically, the closing calls are a demo call, so, like, they're all repetitive, we go through the product.
Discovery is more of a Q&A and, like, what you're paying, what you're trying to solve, talk a little bit about yourself.
Yeah, that's definitely where we'd find those insights, I think, because the closing is definitely going to be answering those questions, and so it would be more inference.
This comes from the horse's mouth, so I'd speak, so I think that's probably better.
Yeah, like, I think the easiest way without doing too much work is to just give you access to this page.
Okay, cool.

just because that's so fluid and it's in constant change that whatever we put out in a blog post and we forget to go back and edit it, it's just going to cause a bunch of headaches.

yourself, some advice.

you.

And then Scrunch takes those prompts and it pings different LLMs and it analyzes those responses and then it provides all of that information in a dashboard like this.

LLMs.

I mean, we'll share them live.

I think we can pull, like, the most interesting information.

Got it.
Okay.

let's see.
Yeah.

I'm curious if, like, you can see if it has a preference for the nature of the content.
And then the last one that we would love to see, those are kind of, like, helping us optimize, uh, inputs.
But the other thing I'd be curious to see is sort of a lagging indicator is, um, how is Monograph showing up in questions that don't relate to software?
So if, uh, if someone has a question about their business.
Like a business situation that doesn't mention software, is Monograph somehow showing up as the solution to that because it enables a business practice?
I'd love to be getting more footprint in that way.
So anyways, those are like three areas that I'd love to know more about from this.
Yeah, I think with the prompts that we have right here, we can expand them and include prompts that are more, that aren't software related specifically.
So then we can start to measure that over time.
We can, Scrunch can integrate with Google Analytics data.
So that's something that we can consider, like we can look into setting this up for you.

See the pages that it's referencing and pulling from.
Great.
Cool.
Yeah.
I'll just throw in a couple of observations there.

That's essentially what these prompts are, right?

So if you do have sources that we can do that work on that you know are authoritative, where engineers are collecting and asking questions, and you want to focus on those kinds of prompts that they're asking for, obviously architects too, but, you know, mentioned that engineering is a focus right now.

You can either give us the source and we can scrape those together or if you do any internal exercises that also feed into other motions like sales and you're trying to figure that out for yourself and you want to share those findings with us, please do, because we can add clusters of prompts here to track.
Same thing, as you mentioned, Chris, with the clusters of prompts that are about non-software questions or non-software topics.
We can just add those clusters here.
I also wanted to point out or, like, I guess, expect...
This is like one step above tea leaves at the moment for everyone.
I just want to like put that out there.
We're using scrunch because it's an option for us right now to monitor in a raw way.
Hey, if we throw these prompts, which we are essentially guessing at, at the wall, what comes back?
And it can give us a directional tracking.
So don't go crazy, like looking at these numbers and worrying about like a day-to-day monitoring of these.
This is very much a directional thing at the moment.
I just wanted to put that out there because this is an insanely new science, you know, the geo tracking.
And there are a couple of companies out there, scrunch is one of them that are doing something.

And we have found some success with it, which is great.

you.

So I'm excited.

Great.
a heads up that I'm going to be out on vacation for the next week.

And starting to position us in a more aggressive.
position against PE-owned competitors.

How's the support team?

And what's the vision for the business?

that

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync to discuss content strategy, updates, and improvements for Monograph's marketing efforts.](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=11.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Four new blog posts to be published this week after incorporating feedback](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [GrowthX team granted access to Monograph's Gong call recordings for deeper audience insights](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)
  - [Scrunch dashboard set up to track Monograph's LLM visibility, showing 23% overall presence](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=1001.0)
  - [Monograph to refine positioning as "built by architects for architects" and highlight advantages over PE-owned competitors](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=1606.0)

## Topics

### Content Update and Feedback

  - [Four new blogs delivered last week, to be published after incorporating feedback](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [Avoid mentioning specifics about onboarding or data migration processes in content](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)
  - [Gary provided detailed feedback in Google Docs, including spelling corrections for competitor names](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)
  - [Jo is handling the changes and incorporating feedback into artifacts](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=835.0)

### Audience Insights Collection

  - [GrowthX team granted access to Gong call recordings for engineering-specific insights](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)
  - [Chris to set up generic login for GrowthX team to access Gong system](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=650.0)
  - [Aim to analyze about a dozen calls for pain points and competitor comparisons](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=281.0)

### Scrunch Dashboard Implementation

  - [Dashboard tracks LLM visibility across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=942.0)
  - [380 prompts used so far, generating almost 2,000 responses](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=997.0)
  - [Monograph shows 23% overall presence, with largely positive sentiment](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=1008.0)
  - [Plans to integrate Scrunch data with Looker dashboard for client access](https://fathom.video/share/htWnJpGfAmdX_xEKnxka5CKszGWRkhnT?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)

### LLM Optimization Strategies
